referendum as part of action resolution; after-resolution play must follow it

{Abyssal Hunter} restates its own printed burn-trigger condition (ranged aggravated damage at close range); fails transfer — no other card's ruling changes. Nearest 4.4, but that owns damage mechanics generally, not this card's own vulnerability text

{Admonitions, The} control-retention rule keyed to being the sole controller of a Sabbat vampire; fails transfer — bespoke to this card's own control-rotation mechanism. Nearest 6.3, but that owns control-taking generally, not this specific retention condition
reduced to zero blood trigger is source-agnostic; pairs with R0052's "must be an actual loss" to define the "reduced to X" wording template

discard-phase effect ordering vs. ally return; taxonomy flags this exact ruling as the disputed 6.9/5.5 boundary case
template recurs verbatim at Abandoning the Flesh.1 (R0008)

{Ashur Tablets} confirms play is legal despite an empty ash heap; fails non-obviousness (no stated precondition to violate) and transfer (specific to this card's own lack of a precondition). Nearest 1.11
card effects gated on the vampire being Laibon; fails non-obviousness — a clan/type gate is expected card text, not a general rule. Nearest 1.6
age restriction is this card's own conditional clause tied to controlling a ready Lasombra; fails transfer — no broader age-comparison rule taught. Nearest 5.1
restates the basic "no legal target, no play" rule; fails non-obviousness — any fluent judge knows this. Nearest 3.1
REVERSAL of earlier rulings per the ruling's own text; labeled as currently stated
general arithmetic-order principle for ability values with an inherent floor/minimum (apply modifiers before the floor) — distinct from 1.7's cost-arithmetic order, which is the nearest kin
cannot affect equipment no longer in play; same "no legal target" rule as R0119. Nearest 3.1
confirms {Change of Target} carries no age restriction after this card; fails transfer — a fact about Change of Target's own printed text, not a rule that changes other rulings. Nearest 3.5
describes this card's own guess-the-vampire mechanic; fails transfer — no other card shares this guessing mechanism. Nearest 1.2 (only the "each of their turns" periodicity is generic)

negates the base "predator draws on oust" consequence; this is Bitter and Sweet Story's own printed override — fails transfer, no other card is affected. Nearest 6.5, which owns oust consequences generally, not this card's specific exception.

torpor-rescue burning all such clot counters remains homeless (nearest 5.3); the stacking half (multiple clot counters cumulative on one vampire) is now owned by 1.15, replacing the old 1.2 near-miss.

the stay-or-burn choice is Blade Clot's own counter mechanic — fails transfer; "allies cannot go to torpor" restates a rule any fluent judge already knows — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 5.3 (torpor) and 5.5 (allies).

Chain of Command's own crypt-return condition when its temporary vampires can't bleed; fails transfer — no other card shares this specific requirement-then-return mechanic. Nearest 6.2 (temporary control) but that section doesn't own this return condition.

Card-specific clarification of what Chameleon may target (own vampires, to grant Anarch sect); fails transfer — answers only this card's own targeting legality. Nearest 5.1 (vampire trait changes), which doesn't own targeting scope.

Charming Lobby's own consequence of {Delaying Tactics} (card returns to hand, others barred from the action); fails transfer — the "others forbidden" clause is this card's printed text, not a general rule. Nearest 3.5 (canceled/failed action consequences).
Charnas' own trigger condition (does nothing to an empty vampire that unlocks-in-torpor); fails transfer — specific to Charnas' blood-emptiness check. Nearest 5.3 (torpor state).

Card-specific note on which counter types Church of the Order of St. Blaise may place on locations; fails transfer — no general principle about counters, just this card's compatibility. Nearest 1.12 (cards on cards / counters on hosts).

Restates that this strike-type effect only functions at close range, per the cited rulebook resolve-strike rule; fails non-obviousness — a judge fluent in the rulebook already knows this. Nearest 4.2 (range).
Card-specific numeric-allocation constraint of Conservative Agitation's point distribution; fails transfer — no other card shares this exact allocation clause. Nearest 3.7.5 (referendum procedure).
Restates that you may allocate more points than a target has pool, mirroring the well-known bleed-for-more-than-pool rule; fails non-obviousness. Nearest 6.5 (pool).

Confirms Consignment to Duat may target a vampire with no blood; fails non-obviousness — targeting "a vampire" never implicitly requires blood > 0 absent card text saying so. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).
Card-specific clan requirement (must not control a ready Nagaraja) gating Consume the Dead's burn option; fails transfer — specific to this card's own printed condition. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).

Reminder that Coterie Tactics does not override the normal prey/predator/target restrictions; fails non-obviousness — restrictions apply by default absent an overriding card text. Nearest 3.1 (targets).
Card-specific requirement gating Council of Seraphim's burn option (all Seraph in torpor); fails transfer — specific to this card's own group of vampires. Nearest 5.3 (torpor state).

Restates that an "or" clan requirement (Tremere or Tremere antitribu) is satisfied by either; fails non-obviousness — reading "or" in a requirement clause is the ordinary meaning. Nearest 1.6 (requirements).
Card-specific restriction (unusable if the action that triggered it also ousted you); fails transfer — tied to this card's own printed condition. Nearest 6.5 (ousting).

mechanics of Dam the Heart's River's own cost trigger (blood payment for a maneuver-strike chosen when range is set); fails transfer — no other card conditions a cost this way. Nearest 1.7, which owns cost timing generally but not this card's own trigger.

restates Deep Cover Agent's own burn-option condition ("all your Seraphs in torpor" = "no ready Seraph"); fails transfer — no other card in this corpus shares this exact condition. Nearest 5.7 (Seraph as special class), which doesn't own ability-activation conditions.

!TENSION:no-effect-plays clarifies the burn-option condition is exactly as printed (no ready Seraph), independent of whether a valid target exists — an ability-activation instance of the no-effect-plays question, same polarity as Draba.3 (permitted despite no effect).

restates Derange's own targeting restriction (cannot move it to a vampire already of the target clan); fails non-obviousness — follows directly from the card's printed clan-change effect. No section owns single-card targeting restrictions like this; nearest 1.6.

Diabolic Lure's own pact-counter/pool-burn mechanic; fails transfer — the "counters reach capacity" condition is unique to this card's pact system. Nearest 1.7 (costs), which doesn't own pact-specific triggers.

restates that Diamond Thunderbolt (a cancel effect) does not also extend a temporary control effect's duration; fails non-obviousness — a cancel card is not an extension effect, so this follows directly from what the card does. Nearest 6.2, which doesn't own this specific non-interaction.

restates the bracket-notation convention that a capitalized discipline symbol requires superior level; fails non-obviousness — any judge fluent with discipline-level notation already knows this. Nearest 1.6.

specific incompatibility between Echo of Harmonies' retained political action and Charming Lobby; fails transfer — no other card pairing is affected by this restriction. Nearest 3.7.5, which owns referendum procedure generally but not this specific card interaction.

specific numeric interaction between Eldest Command Undeath and Voter Captivation (counter split 2/0); fails transfer — the split amounts are this card pairing's own text. Nearest 3.7.5 (referendum) / 6.5 (pool), neither of which owns this specific interaction.
card's own ability requires being in play/ready; obvious a burned/ash-heaped card can't act — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.5, adds nothing new.

confirms the chosen card is visible to all; ash heaps are already fully public zones, so this is obvious — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.11.
cannot burn a vampire no longer in play; obvious you cannot affect what isn't there — fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.10.

ordering between Follow the Blood and Perfectionist named by card — fails transfer, depends on this exact pairing rather than a reusable rule. Nearest 2.4.
